content.description:
# Doc AST: Migration Plan

**Status:** Planning (March 2026)

Plan for converting all items with markdown descriptions to `:doc` AST format in-place on the `doc-ast-editor` branch. This includes notes and all other item types (code, libraries, views, type definitions) that have `content.description` fields. The branch becomes the sole note format — merge to master only after migration is complete.

See [Parity Roadmap](item://c0c0c0c0-005e-0000-0000-000000000000) for the feature work that preceded this.

## Scope

| Category | Count | Automation |
|----------|-------|------------|
| All items with string description | 294 | |
| Notes (markdown) | 87 | Bulk convert |
| Notes (already AST) | 3 | — |
| Non-note items (code, views, libs, etc.) | 207 | Bulk convert |
| With tables | 60 (46 notes + 14 other) | Converter update needed |
| With `![](item://...)` transclusions | 23 (16 notes + 7 other) | Converter update needed |
| With `?call=` transclusions | 10 | Manual (eval blocks) |
| With `?symbol=` transclusions | 9 | Converter update needed |
| With `{{field-ref}}` | 5 | Converter update needed |
| With ` ```query ``` ` blocks | 11 | Manual (each is unique code) |

## Prerequisites

### 1. Table support

**Status:** Not started

Tables appear in 46 of 87 notes — this is blocking. Need:

- **AST node type:** `:table` with `:thead`, `:tbody`, `:tr`, `:th`, `:td` children
- **Readonly renderer** ([field-view-doc](item://d0d0d0d0-0003-0000-0000-000000000000)): Render `:table` nodes as HTML tables
- **Converter** ([markdown-to-doc-ast](item://d0d0d0d0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000)): Parse markdown tables
- **Editable view** ([field-view-doc-editable](item://d0d0d0d0-0007-0000-0000-000000000000)): Edit support — could start as non-editable blocks (like code blocks/eval blocks) with structural editing later

### 2. Converter updates

Update [markdown-to-doc-ast](item://d0d0d0d0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) before bulk run:

- `{{field-ref}}` → `["get-field", ":self", ":field"]` (currently emits old `[:field-ref]` format)
- `![](item://id)` → `["transclude", "@id", ":description"]`
- `![](item://id?symbol=name)` → `["transclude", "@id", ":code", ":symbol", {s: "name"}]`
- `![text](item://id?call=fn&params)` → leave as-is for manual conversion (each has unique params)
- Markdown tables → `:table` AST nodes

## Execution Plan

### Phase 1: Prepare
1. Implement table rendering (readonly + converter)
2. Update converter (new expression forms, transclusions, tables)
3. Table editing in prose editor (at minimum: non-editable block treatment)
4. Backup all item JSONs

### Phase 2: Bulk Convert
5. Run converter on all 294 items with string descriptions via Node.js script
6. Eyeball key notes for basic conversion errors
7. Fix any converter bugs, re-run on affected notes

### Phase 3: Manual Fix-up
8. Convert `?call=` transclusions (10 notes) → eval blocks with `transclusion-chrome` + `call-export`
9. Convert ` ```query ``` ` blocks (11 notes) → eval blocks with Hob code
10. Review and fix edge cases found during eyeballing

### Phase 4: Clean Up
11. Remove markdown view code (or keep as fallback?)
12. Update documentation items to reflect the new format
13. Merge to master

## Open Questions

- **Table editing:** Full structural editing (add/remove rows/cols, cell editing) or start with non-editable block treatment? Non-editable is faster to implement but tables are common enough that editing friction would be noticeable.
- **Query block conversion:** Each ` ```query ``` ` block contains unique JS code. Some may have Hob equivalents, others may need `call-export` or JS interop. Assess case-by-case.
- **Markdown fallback:** Keep markdown views available after migration, or remove? The roadmap says keep indefinitely, but if all notes are AST, the views become dead code.

## References

- [Doc AST: Parity Roadmap](item://c0c0c0c0-005e-0000-0000-000000000000) — Feature work completed before migration
- [markdown-to-doc-ast](item://d0d0d0d0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) — The converter
- [field-view-doc](item://d0d0d0d0-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) — Readonly renderer
- [field-view-doc-editable](item://d0d0d0d0-0007-0000-0000-000000000000) — Prose editor
